unexpected_finds.title: Verdict re-derived 'Searcher's Fatigue' failure-mode flag from Mathlib-specific reasoning
unexpected_finds.description: Slot 144 prompt did not cite the project's existing 'Searcher's Fatigue' / fabricated-math failure-mode memory; principal synthesizer independently identified the same failure mode in Q5(c) reasoning ('Producing sorry-discharge candidates risks the agent introducing fabricated math (the AEAL-relevant failure mode from prior session memory: Searcher's Fatigue)'). Convergence between independent synth witness and stored project memory is positive forensic signal for the project's AEAL discipline -- the failure-mode pattern is robust across contexts.
unexpected_finds.interpretation: Confirms slot 141C scope tightening (Change 2) is well-founded on multi-source evidence: (a) project's own AEAL discipline memory, (b) principal synth's independent re-derivation. Strengthens the no-discharge-candidates exclusion rule.

unexpected_finds.title: Synth recommended Phase B reorder despite cascade-132 procedural validity
unexpected_finds.description: Cascade-132 (`fd669d3`) substrate explicitly authorizes RULE 1 lift on documented-commitment grounds before M10 V0 math is meaningfully advanced (sec 5: 'Operator-discretion permits lift before M10 with documented commitment.'). Slot 144 verdict Q2 acknowledged the procedural validity but recommended Option (a) reorder (fire AFTER C.3) anyway, on audit-trail-narrative grounds rather than procedural grounds. This is a stronger amendment than the procedural-validity precedent strictly required.
unexpected_finds.interpretation: Synth applied a non-procedural narrative-strength criterion to a procedurally-valid action. The amendment is well-justified (commitment-paragraph evidence is currently thinner than cascade-132 assumed given iteration-13 active fix loop) but does represent an extra-procedural standard the operator may want to re-evaluate. Documented for transparency; CLI agent absorbed without contest.

unexpected_finds.title: Risk R1 (commitment-fill drift) probability classified HIGH despite no historical data
unexpected_finds.description: Slot 144 verdict Q6.1 classifies risk R1 (operator commitment-fill drift) as HIGH probability / LOW severity. The HIGH probability classification is asserted without substrate-citation; it appears to be a synth-side prior on operator behavior rather than evidence-based. The mitigation (time-box; escalate to synth for paragraph-drafting consultation) is sound regardless of the probability classification.
unexpected_finds.interpretation: Classification holds at face value as a prudential prior. Operator may want to re-evaluate the probability classification once Phase A fires (data point for future risk-register updates). No action required for current absorption.
